title: Dockerfile Frontend, Build Context, Instructions, and Process Contracts · Docker Systems Brief: Dockerfile From Copy Add Workdir
summary: A small accessible web application needs a readable Dockerfile whose build inputs, defaults, process model, and runtime files are explicit. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dockerfile from copy add workdir increment in reviewed application Dockerfile and build-context contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use FROM, WORKDIR, COPY, and justified ADD behavior with explicit ownership, paths, checksums, and context boundaries.
Define local, Git, named, empty, and remote build contexts and use Dockerfile-specific ignore rules to minimize authority and invalidation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example build sends a 780 MiB context containing .git and local.env, uses a floating base, copies the repository, runs as root, and wraps the process in /bin/sh. Redesign context and runtime contracts. Competency probe: Use FROM, WORKDIR, COPY, and justified ADD behavior with explicit ownership, paths, checksums, and context boundaries. A defensible solution applies Dockerfile From Copy Add Workdir, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to reviewed application Dockerfile and build-context contract.

content.code:
# Worked focus: dockerfile-from-copy-add-workdir — Use FROM, WORKDIR, COPY, and justified ADD behavior with explicit ownership, paths, checksums, and context boundaries.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-dockerfile-build-context-instructions-4
  - competency: docker-worked-dockerfile-build-context-instructions-4
    artifact: Dockerfile
    decision: worked dockerfile build context instructions 4
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      FROM docker.io/library/alpine:3.22@sha256:verified AS runtime
      WORKDIR /app
      COPY --link public/ ./public/
      USER 10001:10001
      ENTRYPOINT ["/app/status"]
    verify: docker buildx build --check --progress=plain .
    failure: Reject a floating base, secret-bearing context, shell-form signal wrapper, root runtime, or copied repository wildcard.
    repair: Pin the reviewed base digest, shrink .dockerignore context, use explicit COPY, set numeric non-root identity, and test argv behavior.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
